breakdown: Root: to speak. vācā: speech, utterance.

vādita.gram: neuter noun (past participle of vādeti used as noun)
breakdown: Root: to sound, speak. Causative vādeti: to play (an instrument).
Past participle suffix; vādita: what is played — instrumental music.

vādita instrumental.summary: A glossary fragment; the entry is simply the word vādita, “instrumental music”.
breakdown: Instrumental music (past participle of vādeti, to play).

vāhaṁ.meaning: and I (va/eva + ahaṁ); or: carrying, a load
vāhaṁ.gram: sandhi fusion (va + ahaṁ) as glossed in the lexicon
breakdown: I (first person pronoun). Sandhi: va + ahaṁ > vāhaṁ.

vālabījaniyā.meaning: a chowrie; a fan or whisk made of a yak's tail

vārigocaro.meaning: dwelling in water; having water as its range (said of a fish)
breakdown: Range, pasture, dwelling-place (go “cow” + cara “roaming”: grazing ground).

vāritta.meaning: avoidance, abstinence (what is to be avoided)
breakdown: Root: to obstruct, ward off. vāritta: avoidance — the side of morality consisting in abstaining.

vāsessanti.meaning: they will make dwell; they will cause to live (somewhere)
vāsessanti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural future, causative of vasati (√vas)
breakdown: Root: to dwell. Causative stem vāse: to cause to dwell.

vātehi.gram: masculine noun, instrumental plural of vāta

vāto.gram: masculine noun, nominative singular (√vā, to blow)

breakdown: Prefixes: contracted to vāya- before the root.
Root: to restrain, exert. vāyamati: to strive, endeavour.
Root: to exert. vāyāma: effort — as in sammāvāyāmo, right effort.

vīmaṁsāya.gram: feminine noun, instrumental singular of vīmaṁsā
breakdown: Investigation, examination (desiderative formation from √man, to think).

vīriyamārabhato.meaning: of one putting forth energy; for one undertaking exertion
vīriyamārabhato.gram: present participle phrase, genitive/dative singular (vīriyaṁ + ārabhato)
breakdown: Energy, vigour (accusative vīriyaṁ; the -m- is sandhi).
Undertaking, putting forth (present participle of ārabhati; genitive ārabhato).

vītamaccharo.meaning: free from miserliness; devoid of stinginess
breakdown: Free from, devoid of (past participle of vi + √i, gone away).
Root: to step. vītikkama: overstepping — transgression.

vītikkamitabbavatthusamāyoge.meaning: when there is occasion combining with an object that could be transgressed against; in the combination of circumstances for transgression
breakdown: To be transgressed (future passive participle of vītikkamati).

vītisāretvā.meaning: having exchanged (greetings); having addressed, having made (courteous talk) pass between
breakdown: Root: to go, flow. Causative sāre: to make pass.

vītivatto.meaning: having overcome, gone through, passed beyond
vītivatto.gram: past participle of vītivattati (vi + ati + √vatt), masculine nominative singular
breakdown: Root: to turn, proceed. vītivatta: gone beyond, overcome.

vūpasamā.meaning: calmness, tranquillity; from the calming, with the subsiding (of)
breakdown: Root: to be calm. vūpasama: calming, subsiding, tranquillising.

yadajjhagā.gram: sandhi fusion: relative pronoun + aorist (yaṁ/yad + ajjhagā)
breakdown: Which (relative pronoun yaṁ, in its sandhi form yad-).

yadidaṁ.meaning: such as, namely, that is to say
yadidaṁ.gram: indeclinable sandhi fusion (yad + idaṁ, “which is this”)

yamakavaggo.meaning: the Yamakavagga, the “Chapter of Pairs” (the first chapter of the Dhammapada)
yamakavaggo.summary: So called because its verses are arranged in contrasting pairs (yamaka, “twin”).

yampicchaṁ.meaning: whatever is desired; what is wished for
yampicchaṁ.gram: sandhi fusion: pronoun + particle + adjective (yaṁ + api + icchaṁ)
breakdown: Even, also (api; the -p- doubles by sandhi).

yantaṁ.gram: sandhi fusion of relative and demonstrative pronouns (yaṁ + taṁ)

yasañca.gram: noun (yasa, mano-group), accusative singular + enclitic ca

yasmā.gram: indeclinable conjunction (ablative of relative pronoun ya)

yassekarattimpi.gram: sandhi phrase: genitive pronoun + numeral + noun + enclitic (yassa + eka + rattiṁ + pi)

yassānubhāvato.meaning: by whose power; through the might of which
yassānubhāvato.gram: sandhi phrase: genitive pronoun + noun in ablative (yassa + anubhāvato)
breakdown: Power, might, majesty (ablative anubhāvato, “by the power”).

yassāṇaṁ.gram: sandhi phrase: genitive pronoun + noun (yassa + āṇaṁ)

yathindakhīlo.meaning: just as Indra's pillar; like the firm city-gate post
yathindakhīlo.gram: sandhi phrase: adverb + compound noun (yathā + indakhīlo)
breakdown: Pillar, post. indakhīla: the firmly planted post at a city gate — an image of unshakeability.

yathā just.summary: A glossary fragment; the entry is simply the word yathā, “just as”.

yathābhuccato.meaning: in accordance with reality; as it really is, in this case

yathābhūtaṁ.meaning: as it really is; in accordance with reality
yathābhūtaṁ.summary: Literally “as become”: seeing things as they have come to be — as they really are.
yathābhūtaṁ.gram: adverbial compound (yathā + bhūta, past participle), accusative singular
breakdown: Become, real (past participle of bhavati). Together: as it has become — as it really is.
yathābhūtaṁ.goenka: Yathābhūta is central to how this tradition describes Vipassana: observing reality as it is, not as one would like it to be. The literal sense “as become” points to direct experiential observation — the meditator examines whatever arises in mind and body just as it actually is, with equanimity, rather than through beliefs or imagination.

yathākāmakāritaṁ.meaning: acting as one desires; doing whatever one wishes (with someone)

breakdown: Worthy, fitting. Together: according to what is fitting — duly.

yatthannapānaṁ.gram: sandhi phrase: adverb + nouns (yattha + annaṁ + pānaṁ)

yatvādhikaraṇamenaṁ.meaning: for which reason... him; on account of which (this person)
yatvādhikaraṇamenaṁ.summary: A sandhi fusion from the sense-restraint formula: “for which reason (covetousness etc. might invade) him”.
yatvādhikaraṇamenaṁ.gram: sandhi fusion: adverb + neuter noun + pronoun (yato + va + adhikaraṇaṁ + enaṁ)
breakdown: From which, because (sandhi yato + va > yatvā-).
Reason, cause; here adverbial: “by reason of which”.

yañhi.gram: sandhi fusion: relative pronoun + particle (yaṁ + hi)

yaṁnūnāhaṁ.meaning: what if I...; suppose I... (introducing a deliberation)
yaṁnūnāhaṁ.gram: sandhi fusion: pronoun + particle + pronoun (yaṁ + nūna + ahaṁ)
breakdown: I. Together: “suppose now I were to...”.

ye.gram: relative pronoun, masculine nominative plural of ya

yena.meaning: by which; whereby; (with verbs of going) towards where
yena.gram: relative pronoun, instrumental singular of ya

yiṭṭhaṁ.meaning: sacrificed, offered; what is offered in sacrifice
yiṭṭhaṁ.gram: past participle of yajati (√yaj), neuter nominative/accusative singular
breakdown: Root: to sacrifice, make an offering.

yobbanasmiñca.gram: neuter noun, locative singular + enclitic ca
breakdown: Youth, the prime of life (from yuva, young).
yobbanasmiñca.note: divergence: the lexicon line parses asmiṁ as “I”, but -smiṁ here is the locative singular ending; the sense is “and in youth”.

yogakkhemakāmā.meaning: desiring safety from bondage; wishing for the peace of release from the yokes
breakdown: Safety, peace. yogakkhema: safety from bondage (the kk is junction doubling).

yogo.meaning: yoke, bond; application, devotion (to a practice); in the precept compound, being addicted to (intoxicants)
yogo.gram: masculine noun, nominative singular (√yuj, to yoke)
breakdown: Root: to yoke, join. yoga: bond, attachment; also application, being devoted or addicted to.
yogo.note: divergence: the lexicon's gloss line here explains the whole precept compound surāmerayamajjappamādaṭṭhānānuyogo (“being addicted to fermented and distilled intoxicants, an occasion for carelessness”), of which (anu)yogo is the final member meaning “being addicted to, applying oneself to”.
